title: Differentiation Using Chain Rule
description: Process for finding derivatives of composite functions
mermaid:
graph TD A1[Start: Identify Composite Function] style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#ffffff B1[Define Outer Function f(u) and
Inner Function g(x)] style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000000 C1["Calculate Derivative of
Outer Function f'(u)"] style C1 fill:#51cf66,color:#ffffff D1["Calculate Derivative of
Inner Function g'(x)"] style D1 fill:#51cf66,color:#ffffff E1[Substitute g(x) back into f'(u)
to get f'(g(x))] style E1 fill:#74c0fc,color:#ffffff F1["Apply Chain Rule Formula:
f'(g(x)) * g'(x)"] style F1 fill:#51cf66,color:#ffffff G1[Simplify the Resulting
Expression] style G1 fill:#51cf66,color:#ffffff H1[Final Derivative:
dy/dx = f'(g(x)) * g'(x)] style H1 fill:#b197fc,color:#ffffff I1["End"] style I1 fill:#b197fc,color:#ffffff A1 --> B1 B1 --> C1 B1 --> D1 C1 --> E1 D1 --> F1 E1 --> F1 F1 --> G1 G1 --> H1 H1 --> I1
entities:
Define Outer Function f(u) and
Inner Function g(x)

Substitute g(x) back into f'(u)
to get f'(g(x))
